WorkoutDescription: STRENGTH SESSION: 3x12 back squats, 3x10 walking lunges, 3x10 step-ups (each leg), 3x15 calf raises, 3x30s plank, 3x12 dead bugs. Rest 60-90s between sets.

Details: Flat or rolling terrain, finish with 4x20s strides
WorkoutDescription: EASY RUN: 45min at conversational pace (130-145 bpm). Should feel easy - slow down if HR drifts above 145. STRIDES: 4x20s gradual acceleration to 90% max speed (Z5 ~181+), walk back recovery. Focus on smooth form, not all-out sprinting.

TEMPO SESSION: 10min warm-up building from Z1 to Z2. Main set: 3x10min at tempo pace (HR 150-165, feels comfortably hard, can speak short sentences only). 2min easy jog between intervals. 10min cool-down in Z1. Tempo should feel sustainable but challenging.

RECOVERY STRENGTH: 3x10 push-ups, 3x10 rows, 3x12 shoulder press light, 3x20 Russian twists, 3x30s side plank each side. Finish with 10min mobility/stretching (hip flexors, hamstrings, quads, calves).

Details: Optional light jog or full rest
WorkoutDescription: REST DAY: Full rest preferred. If legs feel good, optional 20-30min very easy jog keeping HR under 130. This should feel like you could do it forever. If in doubt, rest.

LONG RUN: 2 hours at easy aerobic pace. HR should stay 130-145 throughout. Walk uphills if HR pushes above 145. Practice fueling: take 30-40g carbs every 45min (gel, bar, or drink). Stay hydrated. Focus on time on feet, not pace or distance.

RECOVERY SESSION: 45min very easy movement. Can be easy jog (HR under 140) or bike spin (low resistance). Goal is blood flow for recovery, not fitness. Should feel effortless. If legs are tired from Saturday, do bike instead of run.
STRENGTH SESSION: 3x12 back squats (add 2.5-5kg from last week if possible), 3x10 Bulgarian split squats, 3x10 box step-ups (each leg), 3x15 single-leg calf raises, 3x45s plank, 3x15 dead bugs.
EASY RUN: 50min at conversational pace. HR 130-145. Nose breathing should be possible most of the time. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast. This builds your aerobic engine.

HILL REPEATS: 10min easy warm-up. Find a steep hill (8-12% grade). 8x2min hard uphill effort - HR will climb to 165-180+ by end of each rep. Push into Z5 on final 30s if feeling good. Jog/walk down for recovery (HR should drop below 140 before next rep). 10min easy cool-down. Focus: powerful glute drive, quick cadence, forward lean.
RECOVERY STRENGTH: Same as Week 1 Thursday. 3x10 push-ups, 3x10 rows, 3x12 shoulder press, core circuit, 10min mobility.

Details: Optional light jog or full rest
WorkoutDescription: REST DAY: Prioritize rest. If very fresh, optional 20-30min Z1 jog.

LONG RUN: 2h15 on trail with elevation. Keep HR in Z2 (130-145) - this means walking uphills. Practice hiking technique on steep sections: short steps, hands on thighs if needed. Fuel every 45min. Build duration gradually.
RECOVERY: 45min easy. Bike preferred if legs are fatigued from Saturday's hills.
STRENGTH SESSION: Progress weights slightly. 3x10 back squats heavier, 3x10 Romanian deadlifts, 3x10 walking lunges with dumbbells, 3x12 step-ups, core circuit.

Details: Flat or rolling, finish with 5x20s strides
WorkoutDescription: EASY RUN + STRIDES: 50min easy (Z2), then 5x20s strides accelerating to near-max speed (Z5). Full walk recovery between strides. Strides improve running economy and provide brief Z5 stimulus without fatigue cost.

TEMPO SESSION: Longer intervals this week. 10min warm-up. 2x20min at tempo (HR 150-165). 3min easy jog between. 10min cool-down. Mental challenge: stay locked in for full 20min. Pace should feel hard but sustainable.
RECOVERY STRENGTH: Upper body and core focus. Include extra hip mobility work (pigeon pose, 90/90 stretch).

Details: Optional light jog or full rest
WorkoutDescription: REST DAY: Rest preferred before big weekend.
LONG RUN: 2h30 - longest run yet. Stay in Z2. Practice race nutrition: aim for 60g carbs/hour (gel every 30min or equivalent). Note what works and what doesn't. Hydration: 500ml/hour minimum.
RECOVERY: 50min easy. Essential after 2h30 long run. Keep it truly easy.
DELOAD STRENGTH: 50% of normal weight. 2x10 squats light, 2x10 lunges bodyweight, extended mobility work (15min). Recovery week - don't push.
DELOAD RUN: 40min very easy. HR should sit at lower end of Z2 (130-138). Enjoy the easy effort. Body is adapting to previous 3 weeks.
EASY RUN: Another easy day. No tempo, no intervals. Just easy running. This is when fitness actually builds - during recovery.
FULL REST: No exercise. Sleep well, eat well, hydrate.
REST OR EASY: Light 20min jog if feeling restless, otherwise rest.
DELOAD LONG RUN: Only 1h30 this week. Keep it flat and easy. No fueling practice needed for this duration.
RECOVERY: Easy bike or walk. Prepare for next training block.
STRENGTH SESSION: Back to normal load. 3x10 back squats, 3x8 Romanian deadlifts heavier, 3x10 Bulgarian split squats, 3x12 box jumps (power), core circuit.

Details: Flat or rolling, 5x20s strides at end
WorkoutDescription: EASY RUN + STRIDES: 50min Z2 running + 5x20s strides (Z5). Coming off deload week - should feel fresh.

HILL SESSION WITH Z5 SPRINTS: 10min warm-up. 10x2min hill repeats (Z4). Then 4x10s MAXIMAL hill sprints (Z5+, all-out effort, very short). Full 2min recovery between sprints. 10min cool-down. Hill sprints build explosive power and neuromuscular recruitment.
RECOVERY STRENGTH: Upper body focus with extended mobility.

Details: Optional light jog or full rest
WorkoutDescription: REST: Prioritize rest before big weekend.

Details: 1000m+ D+ target - walk uphills to stay in zone
WorkoutDescription: LONG RUN WITH VERTICAL: 2h45 targeting 1000m+ elevation gain. This means finding mountains or repeating a hilly loop. Walk ALL uphills to keep HR in Z2. Practice hiking poles if you'll use them in races. Fuel every 40-45min.
RECOVERY: Essential easy session after big vertical day. Bike may be better for leg recovery.
STRENGTH SESSION: 3x10 squats, 3x8 deadlifts, 3x10 lunges, 3x10 step-ups, core circuit. Progress weights if form is solid.
EASY RUN: 55min at conversational pace. Focus on relaxed form.
TEMPO SESSION: 3x15min tempo intervals. Total 45min at threshold. Challenging session - stay mentally focused. HR 150-165.
RECOVERY STRENGTH: Lighter session with mobility focus.

Details: Optional light jog or full rest
WorkoutDescription: REST: Full rest recommended before 3h long run.

LONG RUN - 3 HOUR MILESTONE: First 3h run. This is race-relevant duration. Stay disciplined in Z2. Practice your exact race nutrition - aim for 60-80g carbs/hour. Note energy levels, GI comfort, hydration needs. Walk breaks are fine.
RECOVERY: Very important after 3h effort. Keep it truly easy.
STRENGTH SESSION: Continue progression. Add eccentric focus for downhill prep: 3s lowering phase on squats and lunges.
EASY RUN + STRIDES: 55min Z2 + 6x20s strides. Strides should feel smooth and fast, not strained.

Details: 10min WU + 40min fartlek (vary effort by feel) + 10min CD
WorkoutDescription: FARTLEK: 'Speed play' - unstructured workout. After warm-up, vary effort by feel: surge for 1-3min (Z4), recover (Z2), sprint a short hill (Z5), jog easy, pick up pace on flat section, etc. 40min of playing with speed. Develops adaptability and race feel. Fun session!
RECOVERY STRENGTH: Standard recovery session.

Details: Optional light jog or full rest
WorkoutDescription: REST: Rest before peak long run week.
LONG RUN WITH VERTICAL: 3h15 with 1200m+ D+. Practice power hiking technique on all steep uphills. Walk poles recommended. Fuel consistently. This simulates race conditions.
RECOVERY: Easy movement to flush legs.
DELOAD STRENGTH: Light weights, extended stretching and foam rolling.
DELOAD RUN: Easy 45min. Body absorbing previous training.
EASY WITH STRIDES: 40min easy + 4x15s strides to keep legs responsive. No hard efforts this week.
FULL REST: Complete recovery day.
REST OR EASY: Light movement only.
DELOAD LONG RUN: 2h easy. Flat preferred. No fueling stress - just easy running.
RECOVERY: Easy bike or walk.
STRENGTH SESSION: Back to full intensity. Final hard strength week before race-specific phase.
EASY RUN + STRIDES: 55min Z2 + 6x20s strides (Z5). Should feel good coming off deload.
THRESHOLD INTERVALS: 5x6min at threshold pace (HR 155-170). Shorter intervals but higher intensity than tempo. Should feel hard but controlled. 2min jog recovery between.
RECOVERY STRENGTH: Standard recovery session.
FULL REST: Save energy for Saturday's big effort.

Details: Big effort - practice race day nutrition
WorkoutDescription: PEAK LONG RUN: 3h30 - biggest run of base phase. Practice EVERYTHING: race nutrition (60-80g carbs/hr), hydration, pacing, gear. This is a dress rehearsal. Stay in Z2 but expect fatigue in final hour.
RECOVERY: Essential after peak long run. Very easy.
STRENGTH: Maintain but don't increase. Preparing for race-specific block.
EASY RUN: Standard Z2 aerobic run.
EASY + STRIDES: Light session. 45min easy + 4x20s strides to stay sharp.
LIGHT STRENGTH: Reduced volume. Extended mobility work.
FULL REST: Prepare for race simulation.

Details: Race simulation - full kit / nutrition / pacing
WorkoutDescription: 4-HOUR RACE SIMULATION: Final base phase test. Wear race kit, use race nutrition plan (60-80g carbs/hr), carry what you'll carry in race. Practice aid station routine. This builds confidence that your systems work. Walk uphills, stay Z2, finish feeling you could go longer.
RECOVERY: End of Phase 1. Congratulations on completing base build! Easy recovery today.
STRENGTH - DOWNHILL PREP: Focus on eccentric strength for descents. 3x10 slow negative squats (4s down), 3x10 walking lunges with 3s negative, 3x10 step-downs (controlled), Nordic curls if able.
EASY + STRIDES: 45min Z2 on trails + 5x20s strides. Get comfortable on technical terrain.
RACE PACE WORK: 4x8min at expected race effort (Z3-Z4). This is harder than tempo - closer to what you'll sustain in the race. 3min jog between. Learn what race pace feels like.
STABILITY STRENGTH: Single leg focus. 3x10 single leg deadlifts, 3x10 single leg squats to box, balance work, core.
REST OR EASY: Rest preferred.
COURSE SIMULATION: 3h30 with 1500m+ D+. If possible, run similar terrain to Balcons d'Azur. Practice race nutrition and pacing strategy.
RECOVERY: Easy recovery session.
STRENGTH MAINTENANCE: Same exercises, same weight. Don't add load this close to race.
EASY RUN: Standard Z2 on trails.
HILL REPEATS: 6x3min hard uphill. Push into Z5 on final minute of each rep. This is last hard session before taper.
EASY RUN: Short and easy. Recovery from Wednesday.
FULL REST: Beginning pre-race rest.

Details: Last big long run before race
WorkoutDescription: FINAL LONG RUN: Last 3h run before Balcons. Keep it easy, don't push. Practice final gear check.
RECOVERY: Easy bike or walk. Taper begins.
TAPER STRENGTH: Very light. 2x8 squats (50% weight), mobility focus. Keep blood flowing, don't create fatigue.
TAPER RUN: Easy 40min. May feel heavy - that's normal during taper.

Details: Keep legs fresh with short accelerations
WorkoutDescription: SHAKEOUT: 30min easy + 4x30s strides (Z5). Strides keep neuromuscular system primed without creating fatigue.
FULL REST: Rest and prepare mentally.
SHORT EASY: 30min easy jog. Legs should start feeling fresh.
SHAKEOUT: 20min easy + 3x20s strides. Keep legs activated.
FULL REST: 6 days to race. Rest, hydrate, carb-load begins.
RACE WEEK SHAKEOUT: 25min very easy. Stay loose.
FULL REST: Focus on sleep and nutrition.
ACTIVATION: 15min easy + 3x20s strides (Z5). Brief efforts keep you sharp.
FULL REST: Two days to race.
REST OR WALK: 15-20min walk if restless, otherwise full rest.
PRE-RACE REST: Prepare gear, lay out nutrition, sleep early.

Details: First ultra test - conservative start - execute nutrition plan
WorkoutDescription: RACE DAY: Balcons d'Azur 68k. Start conservative! First 20km should feel easy. Execute nutrition plan (60-80g carbs/hr from start). Walk all steep uphills. This is a training race - finish strong, learn for Gypaète.
POST-RACE REST: Full rest. Eat well, hydrate, celebrate!

Details: Light walk only if feeling good
WorkoutDescription: ACTIVE RECOVERY: 20-30min walk if legs feel ok. No running.
TEST RUN: 30min very easy. Check how legs respond. Stop if any sharp pains.
FULL REST: Continue recovery.
EASY RUN: If Wednesday felt ok, 35min easy today.
EASY RUN: Building back. Still recovery week - don't push.
RECOVERY: Bike or walk. Prepare for Gypaète build next week.
STRENGTH RETURN: Light weights. 2x10 squats, 2x10 lunges, core work. Ease back in.
EASY RUN: 50min Z2 on trails. Getting back to normal training.
TEMPO: Return to intensity. 2x12min tempo. Should feel manageable.
RECOVERY STRENGTH: Core and mobility focus.
REST OR EASY: Rest preferred.
LONG RUN: 2h easy. First real long run post-race. Don't push - just time on feet.
RECOVERY: Easy session.
STRENGTH: Back to normal. Progress weights slightly.
EASY + STRIDES: 50min Z2 on hilly trails + 5x20s strides.
HILL REPEATS: 8x2min hard uphill. Push into Z5 territory. Mountain race prep.
STABILITY STRENGTH: Single leg focus for mountain stability.
REST: Rest before long run.
LONG RUN WITH VERTICAL: 2h30 with 1000m+ D+. Practice race hiking technique.
RECOVERY: Easy session post-vertical.
STRENGTH - ECCENTRIC FOCUS: Slow negatives for downhill prep. Critical for Gypaète's descents.
EASY RUN: 55min Z2 on trails.
TEMPO: 3x12min at threshold. Building race-specific endurance.
RECOVERY STRENGTH: Core and mobility.
REST: Save energy for Saturday.
LONG RUN WITH VERTICAL: 3h with 1500m+ D+. Gypaète simulation. Practice everything.
RECOVERY: Essential after big vertical day.
STRENGTH: Maintain current level.
EASY + STRIDES: 50min Z2 + 6x20s strides (Z5).
LONG HILL INTERVALS: 4x6min sustained hard uphill effort. Builds climbing endurance. Push to Z5 in final 2min of each.
STABILITY STRENGTH: Single leg and core work.
REST: Big weekend ahead.
PEAK VERTICAL RUN: 3h30 with 1800m+ D+. Biggest vertical week. This is your peak mountain effort before taper.
RECOVERY: Very important after peak effort.
TAPER STRENGTH: Light weights, mobility focus.
TAPER RUN: Easy 45min.
SHARPENING: 5x90s hill repeats (Z4) + 3x10s maximal hill sprints (Z5+). Keeps you sharp without fatigue.
FULL REST: Let body recover.
EASY: Short easy jog.

Details: Last long run - include some hills
WorkoutDescription: FINAL LONG RUN: 2h easy with some hills. Last real training before race.
RECOVERY: Easy bike or walk.
TAPER STRENGTH: Very light. Mobility focus.
EASY: 35min easy.
SHAKEOUT: 25min easy + 4x30s strides (Z5).
FULL REST.
EASY: Very short easy jog.
SHAKEOUT: 15min easy + 3x20s strides.
FULL REST: Race week begins tomorrow.
RACE WEEK SHAKEOUT: 20min very easy.
FULL REST.
ACTIVATION: 15min easy + 3x20s strides.
FULL REST: Two days to race.
REST OR WALK: Very light if needed.

Details: Key mountain race - manage effort on climbs - trust training
WorkoutDescription: RACE DAY: Gypaète 76k. KEY MOUNTAIN RACE. Manage effort on climbs - walk steep sections. Execute nutrition. Trust your training from Balcons. This is your mountain test before Nice.
POST-RACE REST: Full rest. Recover well.
POST-RACE RECOVERY: Full rest day.
ACTIVE RECOVERY: Walk only if feeling ok.
TEST RUN: 30min very easy if legs feel recovered.
FULL REST.
EASY: Run or bike, whatever feels better.
EASY RUN: Building back gently.
RECOVERY: Easy bike spin.
LIGHT STRENGTH: Return to gym. Light weights.
EASY RUN: Stay active.
FLEXIBLE: Run or bike based on how you feel.
REST.
EASY: Standard easy run.
LONG RUN: 1h30 easy. Maintain endurance base.
RECOVERY: Easy movement.
LIGHT STRENGTH: Maintain baseline.
EASY RUN: Standard easy run.
SHORT TEMPO: 15min at threshold. Maintain some intensity.
REST.
FLEXIBLE: Easy movement.
LONG RUN: 1h30-2h if schedule allows.
RECOVERY: Easy.
LIGHT STRENGTH: Baby likely arriving soon. Keep routine.
EASY: Any movement counts.
EASY: Stay flexible with schedule.
REST.
FLEXIBLE: Whatever works.
FLEXIBLE LONG: Do what you can.
REST.

Details: Adapt to baby schedule - any movement counts
WorkoutDescription: SURVIVAL MODE: Baby arriving. Any exercise is a win. Run, bike, treadmill - whatever fits. Don't stress about missing workouts.
SURVIVAL MODE: Treadmill is your friend for quick sessions.
SURVIVAL MODE: Stay consistent when possible.
SURVIVAL MODE: Rest if exhausted. Sleep > training right now.
SURVIVAL MODE: Move when you can.
SURVIVAL MODE: Try to protect one longer session if partner can cover.
REST: Family time.

Details: Baby arrival period - survive and stay active
WorkoutDescription: SURVIVAL MODE: First weeks with newborn. Any movement counts. Don't guilt yourself for missed sessions.
SURVIVAL MODE: Quick treadmill sessions work great.
SURVIVAL MODE: Consistency over intensity.
SURVIVAL MODE: Sleep if you need it.
SURVIVAL MODE: Move when possible.

Details: Protect one longer session if possible
WorkoutDescription: SURVIVAL MODE: Try for one longer session.
REST: Recover.
ADAPTATION: Finding routine. Move when you can.
ADAPTATION: Keep showing up.
ADAPTATION: Any exercise is good.
ADAPTATION: Rest if needed.
ADAPTATION: Movement helps mental health too.
ADAPTATION: Try for longer session this week.
RECOVERY: Light movement or rest.
RETURN TO STRUCTURE: Hopefully routine forming. Starting to rebuild.
EASY: Stay consistent.

Details: Add small intensity if feeling good
WorkoutDescription: FIRST TEMPO BACK: 10min WU + 15min tempo + 10min CD. Only if feeling good. Skip if exhausted.
REST.
EASY: Standard easy run.
LONG RUN: 1h30-2h. Rebuild duration slowly.
RECOVERY: Easy.
STRENGTH RETURN: Back to proper strength work. 3x8 squats, 3x8 lunges, core.
EASY + STRIDES: 40min Z2 + 4x20s strides (Z5). Get neuromuscular system firing again.
TEMPO: 20min at threshold. Rebuilding intensity.
REST.
EASY: Standard easy run.
LONG RUN: 2h. Important for Nice prep.
RECOVERY: Easy session.
STRENGTH: Full return to strength work. 3x10 squats, 3x10 lunges, 3x10 step-ups, core.
EASY + STRIDES: 45min Z2 on trails + 5x20s strides.
HILL REPEATS: 8x2min hard uphill. Push to Z5. Rebuild climbing power.
STABILITY STRENGTH: Single leg work for trail stability.
REST OR EASY: Rest preferred.
LONG RUN: 2h30. Rebuilding distance for Nice.
RECOVERY: Easy session.
STRENGTH: Continue building. Add eccentric work for downhill.
EASY + STRIDES: 50min Z2 + 6x20s strides.
TEMPO: 3x12min at threshold. Building race-specific fitness.
RECOVERY STRENGTH: Core and mobility.
REST: Save for Saturday.
LONG RUN WITH VERTICAL: 3h with 1000m+ D+. Nice course simulation.
RECOVERY: Essential after vertical work.
STRENGTH: Maintain current level.
EASY + STRIDES: Standard easy + strides session.
LONG HILLS + SPRINTS: 4x6min sustained uphill (Z4-Z5) + 4x10s maximal hill sprints (Z5+). Big climbing session.
STABILITY STRENGTH: Single leg and core.
REST: Save for peak long run.
PEAK LONG RUN: 3h30 with 1500m+ D+. Your biggest effort in Nice build. Practice everything.
RECOVERY: Very important after peak effort.
STRENGTH: Last hard strength week. Then taper.
EASY + STRIDES: 50min Z2 + 6x20s strides.
RACE PACE: 35min at Nice race effort. Learn what sub-18h pace feels like. This is ~6km/h average.
LIGHT STRENGTH: Reduce load. Taper approaching.
FULL REST: Save everything for Saturday.

Details: Last big effort - full race nutrition practice
WorkoutDescription: FINAL LONG RUN: 4 hours. LAST BIG EFFORT. Full dress rehearsal: race kit, race nutrition (60-80g carbs/hr), race pacing. Finish feeling confident.
RECOVERY: Very easy. Taper begins now.
TAPER STRENGTH: Light weights. Mobility focus.
TAPER RUN: Easy 45min. May feel sluggish - normal.
SHARPENING: 20min tempo + 4x20s strides (Z5). Keep systems primed.
FULL REST.
EASY: 40min easy.
FINAL 2H RUN: Last longer effort. Keep it easy.
RECOVERY: Very easy.
TAPER STRENGTH: Very light. Extended mobility.
EASY: 35min easy.
SHAKEOUT: 25min easy + 4x30s strides (Z5).
FULL REST.
EASY: Short easy jog. Legs should feel fresh.
SHAKEOUT: 20min easy + 3x20s strides.
FULL REST: Race week begins.
RACE WEEK: 20min very easy shakeout.
FULL REST: Focus on sleep, nutrition, hydration.
ACTIVATION: 15min easy + 3x20s strides (Z5). Brief sharp efforts.
FULL REST: Carb loading. Hydrate well.
REST OR WALK: Light walk if restless.
PRE-RACE: Prepare all gear. Lay out nutrition. Sleep early. Trust your training.

Details: GOAL RACE - Execute plan - sub-18h - trust your training!
WorkoutDescription: GOAL RACE: Nice 110K. Sub-18h target (~6km/h avg). START CONSERVATIVE! First 30km should feel easy. Execute nutrition plan from kilometer 0 (60-80g carbs/hr). Walk all steep uphills. Stay calm through low points - they pass. You've done the work. TRUST YOUR TRAINING!
